name: PhD Resume Sample
summary: Highly motivated biomedical engineering doctoral candidate with eight years of professional experience leveraging artificial neural networks and data science to enhance immune system response mechanisms. Expertise in R programming, MATLAB, and bioinformatics. Seeking a research-intensive role in the biotechnology or pharmaceutical industry to further therapeutic advancements in regenerative medicine.

experience.title: Research Assistant
bullets: Conceive theoretical and data science approaches to map out and analyze immune system response mechanisms based on gene expression

Examine Gene Expression Microarray Datasets from the National Biotechnology Center of Information (NCBI) with R Program and GEO Query computing software to construct clustering data heatmaps, histograms, and Bayesian Test Statistics

Construct a Boolean logic gene circuit to illustrate activated and deactivated genes during immune system response based on data heatmap results to create artificial neural networks for precise therapeutic application

experience.company: Repperger Research Program, United States Air Force Research Laboratory
experience.employer: United States Air Force Research Laboratory
experience.title: Research Intern
bullets: Reverse-engineered older human cadaver head and neck simulation test model to create a new hybrid simulation model
Performed Finite Element Analysis on a new hybrid simulation model using LS-DYNA Software and inputted Cadaver Neck Test Data for model verification & validation
Improved simulation and test methodology for future model projects by proposing the acquisition of updated LS-DYNA software

Summarized results in an MS PowerPoint Presentation and presented to Wright Patterson Air Force Base research staff and U.S. Military Officials during the 2022 U.S Air Force Research Laboratory Summer Research Summit
seniority_signals: Presented to Air Force Base research staff and U.S. Military Officials

experience.company: Louisiana Tech University, Department of Biomedical Engineering
experience.title: Research Assistant
bullets: Assembled capped clay nanotubes combined with targeted stem cell hydrogels for regenerative medicine capabilities with three additional laboratory team members
Investigated novel ways to proliferate and transform stem cells through Cell Staining & Culturing, Macromolecule Probing, and Chemical Distillation and Titration
Articulated that healing factors aligned with influenced stem cells could heal bone deformities and injury sites
Accepted for presentation and publication based on experimentation results at the 2023 Experimental Biology Research Conference and the 2023 Biomedical Engineering Conference

experience.company: Binghamton University, Office of Entrepreneurship & Innovation
experience.title: Technology Transfer Intern
bullets: Examine technology transfer methods and patent portfolio of the entire Watson School of Engineering & Applied Science for strategic planning & execution
Remodel patent portfolio in Microsoft Excel by performing statistical analysis and data visualization
Aid division in discovering new trends such as frequently cited patents and patent life cycle after patent application date

experience.company: Binghamton University, Watson Careers & Alumni Connections Office
experience.title: Graduate Student Assistant
bullets: Address and strengthen resumes and cover letters for undergraduate and graduate students in Engineering & Applied Science through personal appointments with four other student assistants
Archive and transform job and internship postings into newsletters and databases using Microsoft Excel and OmniUpdate
Research information to create new resume templates, contact industry and academic professionals, and execute plans for innovative career advancement for Engineering & Applied Science students

experience.company: Binghamton University, Office of Residential Life
experience.title: Academic Graduate Assistant
bullets: Mentored and tutored over 1,100 undergraduate students to achieve above-satisfactory academic progress within STEM classes such as Biology, Calculus, Physics, and Organic Chemistry
Assembled class study kits and student support information to send to residential students with the help of 15 residential student assistants

Helped plan events that focused on STEM academic support outreach, career expansion, and extracurricular academic participation such as a STEAM Room Opening, Final Exam De-Stress, Discovery Program Tabling, Undergraduate Research Day, and the Job & Career Internship Fair
Improved the personal academic experiences and grade performances of undergraduate students that further led to strengthened career insight, focus, and prospects
seniority_signals: Mentored and tutored over 1,100 undergraduate students

education.institution: Binghamton University, SUNY, Thomas J. Watson College of Engineering and Applied Science
Cornell University, Meinig School of Biomedical Engineering
Huston Tillotson University, College of Natural Sciences
education.field: Biology with a Minor in Mathematics

publications.title: Effect of single and multiple doses of low-level laser therapy on viability and proliferation of stem cells from human exfoliated deciduous teeth (SHED)
